question.text: A trader who buys a futures contract takes a ______ position, and a trader who sells takes a ______ position.

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Buying = Long position (profits when price rises). Selling = Short position (profits when price falls). 'Buy' and 'sell' are figurative — no money or underlying changes hands when the deal is executed. **The Trap:** These terms are universal across all derivatives markets. Don't confuse with opening/closing positions. **Cross-Exam Context:** Long/short terminology is fundamental to all NISM derivatives exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Long USDINR futures = bullish on USD (expect INR depreciation). Short USDINR futures = bearish on USD (expect INR appreciation).
